message:
fix(tools): add cycle detection to process tree traversal to prevent stack overflow

The collect_process_tree() recursive function walks the parent-child
process map to build a kill list for process tree termination. If the
children_map contains a cycle (which can occur due to race conditions
when reading /proc — a child can momentarily appear as its own
ancestor), the recursion would loop infinitely until stack overflow.

Add a HashSet<Pid> visited set to track already-traversed PIDs and
skip them on re-encounter, ensuring termination regardless of
children_map topology.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
